questions.questionText: The most common congenital heart disease is:
questions.optionB: Ventricular septal defect (VSD — 25-30% of all CHD, pansystolic murmur at left lower sternal border)
questions.explanation: VSD: most common CHD overall (25-30%). Left-to-right shunt (acyanotic). Murmur: harsh pansystolic at left lower sternal border. Small VSD: asymptomatic, may close spontaneously. Large VSD: CHF symptoms (failure to thrive, tachypnea, recurrent chest infections), pulmonary hypertension. Eisenmenger syndrome: reversal of shunt (L→R becomes R→L) due to irreversible pulmonary vascular disease → cyanosis. Treatment: small → observe. Large/symptomatic → surgical closure. Most common cyanotic CHD: Tetralogy of Fallot. Reference: OP Ghai — VSD.

questions.optionB: VSD, right ventricular outflow tract obstruction (RVOTO), overriding aorta, and right ventricular hypertrophy
questions.explanation: TOF: most common cyanotic CHD (after neonatal period). 4 components — (1) VSD (large), (2) RVOTO/pulmonary stenosis (determines severity — more obstruction = more cyanosis), (3) Overriding aorta (straddles VSD), (4) RVH (secondary to RVOTO). CXR: boot-shaped heart (coeur en sabot — due to RVH + concave pulmonary segment). Tet spells: episodes of increased cyanosis (crying/feeding → increased R→L shunt). Treatment: knee-chest position (increases SVR), O2, morphine, phenylephrine. Definitive: surgical repair. Reference: OP Ghai — TOF.
